agent_outputs.raw: Defense wins. The timestamp audit confirming submission-time derivation of all missingness indicators resolves the leakage concern. Missingness at application time is a legitimate predictive signal. The workflow dependence is a disclosed scope limitation. Future-quarter frozen-model evaluation is sound. VERDICT: defense_wins

The key distinction is between submission-time missingness (legitimate) and request-triggered or post-decision missingness (leakage). The study's methods and timestamp audit confirm the former applies. Defense wins. VERDICT: defense_wins

Missingness as a predictive feature is valid when (1) the missingness is observed at scoring time and (2) no post-decision information contaminates the indicator. Both conditions are met by the submission-time timestamp audit. The future-quarter holdout and frozen model confirm evaluation integrity. Defense wins. VERDICT: defense_wins

synthesis: All three assessors unanimously find the evaluation valid. The timestamp audit is the critical evidence that resolves the missingness leakage concern. ETD constraint: unanimous defense wins.
all_issues_raised: missingness leakage concern — resolved by submission-time timestamp audit
workflow dependence — disclosed as scope limitation

empirical_test.condition: Audit feature-generation timestamps to confirm all missingness indicators are submission-time; verify future-quarter frozen-model evaluation; confirm fairness audit covers monitored proxies
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Any missingness indicator timestamped after underwriting decision or outcome
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: All timestamps confirm submission-time; no post-decision information; clean future-quarter evaluation
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Most features submission-time but one has incomplete lineage documentation
